nodes.context: Colombian drug trafficking coalition based in Medellín, active in cocaine trade during late 1970s-1980s.
Colombian drug trafficking organization based in Cali, led by the Rodríguez Orejuela brothers.
Anti-Escobar vigilante group formed in 1993, financed by Cali Cartel and led by Castaño brothers.
Colombian drug trafficking family that joined the Medellín Cartel, bringing their smuggling networks.
Colombian drug trafficking enterprise led by Gonzalo Rodríguez Gacha, part of the Medellín Cartel.
Colombian drug trafficking operation led by Carlos Lehder, initially partnered with George Jung, later part of Medellín Cartel.
American drug smuggler who partnered with Carlos Lehder and later directly with Pablo Escobar.
Colombian guerrilla group that attempted to extort and kidnap cartel members, leading to conflict.
Paramilitary organization formed by Medellín Cartel, Cali Cartel, and others to fight kidnappings.

nodes.standard_name: Asociación Campesina de Ganaderos y Agricultores del Magdalena Medio
nodes.original_text_name: Asociación Campesina de Ganaderos y Agricultores del Magdalena Medio
nodes.context: Legal front for paramilitary groups, handling logistics and public relations for MAS.
Criminal debt collection service founded by Pablo Escobar in Envigado, Colombia.
Mexican drug trafficking organization that cooperated with Medellín Cartel in the 1980s.
Group within Medellín Cartel that violently opposed extradition to the US.
Colombian guerrilla group that stormed the Supreme Court in 1985; alleged ties to Medellín Cartel.
Enforcement wing of Medellín Cartel, later became a successor organization involved in drug trafficking.
Paramilitary group that allied with La Oficina de Envigado for drug trafficking operations.
Mexican drug cartel that was an ally of the Medellín Cartel.
Colombian criminal group allied with the Medellín Cartel.
Colombian criminal group allied with the Medellín Cartel.
Criminal organization allied with the Medellín Cartel.
Spanish criminal groups allied with the Medellín Cartel for drug trafficking.

edges.context: Originally had a strategic alliance and smuggling arrangement, sharing distribution hubs in Miami and Houston.
Became perpetual enemies after a territorial dispute, leading to a feud until Medellín's demise in 1993.
Cali Cartel largely financed Los Pepes to fight against Pablo Escobar.
Los Pepes were formed to target and defeat Pablo Escobar and the Medellín Cartel.
The Ochoa family joined the Medellín Cartel, combining their smuggling networks with Escobar's.
Gonzalo Rodríguez Gacha's trafficking enterprise merged with the Medellín Cartel.
Carlos Lehder's operation became part of the Medellín Cartel, providing distribution networks.
Lehder and Jung partnered in prison and later smuggled cocaine together for the Medellín Cartel.
After falling out with Lehder, Jung worked directly with Pablo Escobar and the Medellín Cartel.
The FARC kidnapped cartel members and attempted to extort them, leading to the creation of cartel-funded private armies.
Medellín Cartel was a founding member and funder of MAS.
Cali Cartel participated in forming MAS.

edges.target: Asociación Campesina de Ganaderos y Agricultores del Magdalena Medio
edges.context: ACDEGAM handled logistics and public relations for MAS.
Escobar founded Oficina de Envigado as a debt collection service for the cartel.
Medellín Cartel cooperated with Guadalajara Cartel for logistics in Nicaragua.
Los Extraditables was a faction within Medellín Cartel that opposed extradition.
Alleged but disputed relationship; M-19 stormed the Supreme Court possibly at cartel's behest.
La Oficina de Envigado was founded as an enforcement wing and later became a partial successor to the Medellín Cartel.
La Oficina de Envigado affiliated with the AUC to organize drug trafficking operations.
The Medellín Cartel allied with the Gulf Cartel for drug trafficking.
Los Priscos were allies of the Medellín Cartel.
The Gallón Gang was an ally of the Medellín Cartel.
La Corporación was an ally of the Medellín Cartel.
The Medellín Cartel allied with Galician clans for drug trafficking in Europe.
